top_weaknesses.issue: Rhetorical tics that signal essay-template: 'This is the structural innovation', 'Read that sentence again', 'The asymmetry is the product', 'The constraint is collapsing', 'labor story of the decade'
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace each with concrete cause-effect or one specific implication; cut 'Read that sentence again' entirely

top_weaknesses.issue: Cyberpunk-framing flourish ('The cyberpunk image of low life is not a soup line') and 'Each is shipping today in mainstream productivity suites' assert claims without anchoring to named tools or evidence
top_weaknesses.fix: Name 1-2 concrete white-collar tools (Microsoft Productivity Score, GitHub Copilot review dashboards, etc.) or qualify ('is being marketed and piloted')

top_weaknesses.issue: One paragraph clocks 83 words ('The worker is not managed by a human...') — wall-adjacent on mobile
top_weaknesses.fix: Split at 'A floor supervisor exists, but...'

top_weaknesses.issue: Final H2 'Why this is the labor story of the decade' is thesis-statement-shaped rather than narrative-advancing
top_weaknesses.fix: Rename to something concrete: 'Algorithmic management is moving up the org chart' or 'Management is the durable AI-labor story'

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor text on Doctorow link is the full quoted sentence (overlong); ADAPT anchor is a bare acronym
top_weaknesses.fix: Tighten anchors: 'Cory Doctorow on Amazon's electronic whipping for coders'; 'Amazon ADAPT productivity tracking system'

consensus_summary: Strong escalating narrative (warehouse → union-busting → keyboard) with the most quotable line on the batch. Trim the rhetorical tics, anchor the white-collar 'shipping today' claim with named tools, and this ships.
